entry.title: claude-obsidian
entry.content: Claude + Obsidian knowledge companion. Sets up a persistent, compounding wiki vault (Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern). v1.7 "Compound Vault" + v1.8 methodology modes close 5 of 5 priority gaps from the May 2026 compass artifact. Ships: substrate alignment with kepano/obsidian-skills, default Obsidian CLI transport, hybrid retrieval (contextual prefix + BM25 + cosine rerank per Anthropic's Sept 2024 research), per-file advisory locking for multi-writer safety, pre-commit verifier agent, AND methodology modes (LYT / PARA / Zettelkasten / Generic) for first-class organizational support no other Claude+Obsidian competitor offers. v1.7.x audit closure: every BLOCKER + HIGH + MEDIUM + LOW finding from the v1.7.0 audit is CLOSED or DEFERRED-with-rationale. Optional DragonScale Memory extension (log folds, deterministic addresses, semantic tiling lint, boundary-first autoresearch).

entry.title: screen-mcp

entry.title: os-control-mcp
entry.content: The sanctioned OS 'motor cortex' for an agent on a Linux box: control systemd services/timers, query journald, read host resources/processes, send desktop notifications, drive D-Bus, and manage power (suspend/reboot/poweroff) — all through structured interfaces (systemctl/loginctl/journalctl/busctl), never raw PID hacks. Pure standard library, zero pip deps. A self-preservation guard refuses severing actions on units the agent depends on (dbus, logind, sshd, network, tailscaled, the session, goosed) unless force=true. Ships the MCP server plus a control-os skill. The system-service counterpart to screen-mcp's GUI control.

entry.title: nature-skills
entry.content: A growing collection of Claude-compatible academic workflow bundles for producing work at Nature-journal standard. Covers scientific figures (nature-figure), manuscript prose polishing (nature-polishing), manuscript drafting and methods writing (nature-writing), reviewer-style pre-submission assessment (nature-reviewer), citation retrieval and export (nature-citation), data availability statements and FAIR metadata (nature-data), paper-to-PPTX presentation conversion (nature-paper2ppt), literature search via MCP (nature-academic-search), paper reading and annotation (nature-reader), and peer-review response drafting (nature-response). Future releases planned: statistical reporting, cover letters, and review articles. Rules are derived from primary sources, including published Nature papers, journal author guidelines, and structured writing curricula.

entry.title: beast-mode
entry.content: Follow-through for high-fan-out development. Optimized for the workflow where a human fires off many parallel, well-scoped work streams — the risk isn't the changes, it's forgetting to finish them. beast-mode is the discipline that makes every fired stream land: it always ends in a PR, absorbs review autonomously, arms the merge on green, closes the issues it fixes, hands off what the environment can't do as a runbook issue, and captures what it learned. Pick beast-mode if your failure mode is dropped threads, not bad changes.

entry.title: email-monitor
entry.content: Use when you want unattended Gmail triage on this machine: a heartbeat tick incrementally watches one or more inboxes over read-only IMAP (UID + UIDVALIDITY watermark, BODY.PEEK, X-GM-MSGID dedupe), classifies each new message by acted-on importance via a three-tier gate (L0 rules / L1 cheap scoring / L2 LLM hook), pushes a redacted ASCII-only Discord alert for URGENT/ACTION mail, archives NOISE through the existing label tool, tracks the resulting affairs in the shared schedule-reminder task pool with deadline normalization (NY to UTC, DST-correct), drafts concise compliance-checked replies as review-only Gmail drafts (never auto-sent), and emits a daily digest. Reuses the on-machine IMAP toolchain, reminder base, and Discord relay rather than rebuilding any store, scheduler, or notifier; app passwords stay in DPAPI and mail bodies never leave the local session. Route bulk relabeling to the raw gmail-imap-label tool, generic non-mail reminders to schedule-reminder, and actual sending to the user by hand.

entry.title: pipeline-orchestrator
entry.content: v8.22.0 (2026-07-07, MINOR — hook-unblock pack: 5 anti-deadlock/anti-friction fixes to the enforcement layer, from the 2026-07-07 corrective-telemetry diagnosis (27 triggers; live egg-and-chicken deadlock killed run 2026-07-04-e2e-ruler-recalibration-s2 with 0/320 diff): (T1) ALREADY-DISPATCHED exemption — pendingAlreadyDispatched + dispatchTargetMatches in edit-guard-hook.cjs treat a DISPATCH_REQUEST as satisfied once the target subagent was genuinely spawned (pending_dispatches record from dispatch-record-hook, created_at >= emitted_at, leaf-containment match mirroring isResolutionAgent) — frees all 3 session blockers at once; block between REQUEST and spawn, tamper fail-closed and malformed-shape guard preserved. (T2) ANCHORED exec-window control-plane exemption in dispatch-pending-gate.cjs — a Bash/PowerShell command that IS exactly node …/scripts/exec-window/open.cjs|close.cjs (env-var prefixes tolerated) passes with a live pending; composition (&&, ;, |, backtick, newline, subshell) or the path embedded as another program argument never does (anti-injection pinned by tests). (T3) DETERMINISTIC pending clear — step-ledger-stamp.cjs (PostToolUse:Agent) clearSatisfiedPending removes the returning agent-matching DISPATCH_REQUEST entry from the signed state (withLock + read-verify-refuse-launder, record-step posture), replacing the honor-only controller prose whose omission caused a false 482-min PROTOCOL_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT. (T4) DISK-TRUTH anti-false-timeout — pendingResolvedByEvents: a pending whose matching DISPATCH_RESULT is already recorded in the run protocol-events.jsonl (timestamp >= request) is RESOLVED, wired into findLivePendingBlock AND the cleanup-orphan checkHandshakeTimeouts sweep (implementing its own docstring promise "AND no matching response arrived"); DISPATCH-only scope (GATE/PLAN_MODE stay human-resolved). (T5) STAMP-BY-EVIDENCE — step-ledger-gate.cjs grants the tdd step when gate-decisions.jsonl carries TDD_APPROVAL APPROVED|CONFIRMED (frozen RED tests from a prior session), persists the stamp via recordStep and leaves a STEP_STAMP_BY_EVIDENCE audit event in protocol-events (NOT a new gate — Iron Law preserved: Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED). Tests tests/regression/v8.22.0/F1-F5 (41 scenarios, TDD RED->GREEN per task); suite 258/8 = pre-existing baseline exactly (3x Langfuse + score-writer + F4 v8.8.0 + 3x v8.21.0 frozen RED from the in-flight S2 slice), 0 new failures. Authored via the loopify harness (loop/hook-unblock branch, per-task selective commits); headless loop hit the harness sensitive-file wall on .claude/hooks/** — implementation landed from the interactive session, lesson recorded in loops/hook-unblock/LOOP_DONE.md. Anterior: v8.20.0 (2026-07-02, MINOR — enforcement unblocking pack from the 2026-07-02 hook audit): (1) STOP-TRAP FAIL-OPEN: the three Stop-family gates (stop-gate-hook, e2e-eval-gate, subagent-stop-commit-hook) blocked FOREVER when their continuity counter could not be persisted (signer unavailable / verifyState failing at write time / read-only state or dir) — persist()/withSignedState() now report real commit success and each block site fails OPEN with an audit trace, extending the existing !statePath B5 guard to every unpersistable shape. (2) scope-lock-hook emits the canonical hookSpecificOutput PreToolUse envelope — the old FLAT top-level shape was not honored by the harness, so the refactor scope deny was either ignored or bound without its reason ever reaching the model. (3) PIPELINE_NOT_ARMED / CORRUPT_STATE denies + the arm-writer/session-arm banners rewritten to ONE reachable next action (dispatch Agent(subagent_type: pipeline-orchestrator:core:pipeline-controller)) — the impossible write-sentinel-state-by-hand recipe (which contradicted MARKER_TAMPERED and deepened the block) and the literal {PIPELINE_DOC_PATH}/<cwd> placeholders are GONE. (4) lib/pipeline-arm.cjs HARNESS_OPEN_TAG now also cuts teammate-message/agent-message envelopes — a background subagent report QUOTING /pipeline-orchestrator:pipeline continue in prose armed a phantom CONTINUE run live on 2026-07-02, bricking the session Bash/PowerShell/Agent until the TTL. (5) NEW .claude/hooks/sentinel-state-autosign.cjs (PostToolUse Write|Edit|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit): signs the Bash-less controller freshly-written UNSIGNED run-dir sentinel-state.json (NEVER touches a present signature — invalid = tamper evidence; SKIPS under PIPELINE_HMAC_STRICT; fail-open) — closes STATE_FILE_INIT_FAIL and most corrective-loop noise; zero security delta (unsigned was already tolerated by default). TDD tests/regression/v8.20.0/F1-F5 (26 scenarios RED-to-GREEN incl. a live-repro of the phantom arm). Iron Law: only .claude/hooks/ + lib/ + hooks/hooks.json + tests/; Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 257/5 (same 5 pre-existing). Lockstep all version surfaces. v8.19.3 (2026-07-01, PATCH — arm-classifier: keyword-inferred read-only runs no longer arm [recurring phantom-arm deadlock fix]): a natural-language review/audit/analysis request ('faça revisão adversarial', 'faça uma auditoria', 'análise') was classified FULL/Audit and armed the pipeline-arm-pending marker — but read-only work never opens an exec window, so it hit INLINE_WORK_BLOCKED, aborted, and left an orphan marker that bricked the next task with PIPELINE_NOT_ARMED (observed live in interview-coach 2026-06-30 and 07-01). FIX: lib/pipeline-arm.cjs::writeArmPending now also skips arming for the read-only TYPES (Audit, UX Simulation) when they were INFERRED FROM KEYWORDS (source kw:), while an EXPLICIT --audit/--ux flag stays a deliberate governed run and still arms (preserving the STEP_1_7 direct-entry evidence, v8.16.0). Safe direction per the documented arm trade-off: a missed arm is recoverable (re-invoke); a phantom arm deadlocks. TDD tests/regression/v8.19.3/F1-readonly-types-no-arm (RED 4-fail → GREEN); 2 deliberate v8.7.0 pins updated to the --audit-flag path. Iron Law: only lib/pipeline-arm.cjs + tests/ touched; Gates/Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 252/5 (5 pre-existing Langfuse/env/F4). Lockstep across all version surfaces. v8.19.2 (2026-07-01, PATCH — edit-guard-hook robustness [Cursor-crash hardening, Claude-Code-scoped]): the PreToolUse edit-guard no longer crashes into the opaque external catch ('edit-guard-hook internal error — failing closed') when .pipeline/sessions is present-but-unreadable (a file where a dir is expected → ENOTDIR/EACCES) — getActiveLock + getActiveExecWindow now guard readdirSync and return null instead of throwing, and the CLI external catch surfaces the REAL sanitized error (single-line, printable-ASCII, capped 200) instead of the opaque message, so a genuine failure stays fail-closed but debuggable. Posture UNCHANGED (still deny when the guard cannot evaluate); NO foreign tool-name vocabulary added — cross-runtime tool mapping is the other editor's concern, deliberately out of scope. TDD tests/regression/v8.19.2/F1 (RED 7-fail → GREEN, 8 checks). Iron Law: only .claude/hooks/edit-guard-hook.cjs + tests/ touched; Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 251/5 (5 pre-existing Langfuse/env/F4). Lockstep 9 surfaces. v8.19.1 (2026-06-30, PATCH — arm-marker consume-on-close deadlock fix): the pipeline-arm-pending marker (written at /pipeline invocation, 30-min TTL) was never consumed when a governed run ended deliberately, so a finished code-mutating run's still-live marker kept blocking the NEXT non-pipeline task in the same session with PIPELINE_NOT_ARMED until TTL/restart (REVIEW-ONLY/DIAGNOSTIC never arm — the trigger is a FULL/code run that armed, completed, and was never disarmed). FIX: stop-gate-hook.cjs consumes the marker on its closed-run ALLOW paths (completed/GO-completer/spec-seal/hard_failed-cap/deliberate-inactive) via a new session-aware lib/pipeline-arm.cjs::clearArmPendingForSession — never on a block, on SessionEnd/StopFailure recovery, or on corrupt state. Positive-ownership delete (deletes only when marker+caller session_ids are both present and equal, OR both absent; ambiguous/mismatched preserved for TTL) + tamper verdict delegated to the lib/arm-pending SSOT (a signed-but-invalid marker stays fail-closed). 2 zero-context adversarial rounds (1 HIGH tamper-bypass + 1 CRITICAL cross-session-delete, both fixed) then a final pass CLEAN. TDD tests/regression/v8.19.1/F1 (10 scenarios, all 5 call sites + tamper + ownership). Iron Law: only .claude/hooks/stop-gate-hook.cjs + lib/pipeline-arm.cjs + tests/ touched; Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 250/5 (5 pre-existing). Lockstep 9 surfaces. v8.17.0 (2026-06-28, MINOR — autonomous bugfix run delivers 4 audit-driven fixes + 1 meta-escape) — (AUDIT-001 MED-LOW) `.claude/hooks/stop-hook.cjs` adds tail-read verify after `appendRunLog`: on mismatch, a single `run-log verify mismatch` warn fires on stderr; soft-fail on missing/malformed; exported as `verifyRunLogAppend` for unit testing. (AUDIT-004 CRIT, deployment) new `lib/enforcement-status.cjs::detectEnforcementStatus({hooksDir, expectedCanonicalHook})` probes the install for the canonical execution-gate hook (`pipeline-arm-gate.cjs`); wired into the existing SessionStart `enforcement-surface-verify-hook.cjs` to emit a dedicated `ENFORCEMENT_INACTIVE` protocol-event with HIGH severity when missing — registered in `lib/contracts/e2e-eval.cjs::HYGIENE_EVENT_SEVERITY` at weight 1.0 so an install with the gate missing cannot reach clean ProcessHygiene. (AUDIT-005 HIGH) `lib/audit-read-budget.cjs::buildAuditCorrectiveDescriptor` adds closed-vocabulary machine-readable fields `corrective_action:"dispatch_agent"` + `agent_type:"audit-intake"`; `buildAuditInlineReason` appends a `[corrective_action=dispatch_agent agent_type=audit-intake]` suffix to the prose reason so a parent reading only text can still pattern-match the recovery action. (AUDIT-006 NEW MED) `lib/e2e-evaluator.cjs::scoreRun` short-circuits on aborted runs (`state.abort_reason` non-empty + `pipeline_outcome` starting with `ABORTED_`): drops StepCompletion / GateCoverage / OrderIntegrity (renormalized over ProcessHygiene), emits exactly one AUDIT-class `hygiene_violation` named `PIPELINE_ABORTED` severity=high weight=1.0 — so aborted runs surface as aborts instead of being scored as catastrophic failures. (META — user-authorized chain-tied deadlock escape) `.claude/hooks/skill-frontmatter-parser.cjs::getEnforcementMode` honours the `PIPELINE_AUTONOMOUS_RECOVERY_MODE=true` env flag by DOWNGRADING `deny` to `warn` for the process (never escalates `warn`; case-sensitive `"true"` only; one stderr audit line per process). The flag institutionalizes the user-authorized self-recovery path: when a parent must dispatch multiple agents in sequence under autonomous /goal full autonomy, the chain-tied enforcement converts to WARN so the run can complete instead of deadlocking at the gate. TDD RED→GREEN via tests/regression/v8.17.0/F1-F5 (5 files, 35+ cases); full suite 4 fail (the 4 pre-existing Langfuse/env baseline — F8/F9/F10 langfuse + F2 score-writer — unchanged). Iron Law preserved: only `.claude/hooks/`, `.codex/hooks/`, `lib/` and `tests/` touched; the 35-row Mandatory Gates table + 49-row Gate Registry UNTOUCHED. Lockstep 9 surfaces. v8.16.0 (2026-06-28, MINOR — autonomous bugfix of 2 glitches caught by the v8.15.0 E2E evaluator during the v8.15.1 self-audit run, both deterministic-evaluator findings): (Glitch A — HIGH, missing_step:final) the step-ledger-stamp.cjs PostToolUse:Agent path was already correct on the standard Agent return shape — F1 in tests/regression/v8.16.0/ now PINS that contract (final-validator return → `final` stamped; raw V1 fence string accepted; errored/ungoverned returns rejected), so a future regression to the stamping path is caught immediately. (Glitch B — MEDIUM, BRAINSTORM_EVIDENCE_MISSING) the v7.14.0 STEP_1_7_ROUTING enforcement requires every MEDIA/COMPLEXA/Spec run to log one of {load-existing, dispatch-brainstorm, no-prep-override, simples-bypass}, but the direct-entry /pipeline path never auto-emitted `no-prep-override` so the final-validator hygiene check raised the violation — now `step-ledger-stamp.cjs` auto-emits a single STEP_1_7_ROUTING(no-prep-override) entry the first time a governed step is stamped on a direct-entry run, idempotent + scoped to qualifying complexity/type and skipped when the run already carries a step_1_7 block or a STEP_1_7_ROUTING gate entry. Both fixes land in ONE existing hook (`step-ledger-stamp.cjs`) via the SSOT step-1-7-routing writer (lib/step-1-7-routing.cjs); no new hook, no new gate, the 35-row Mandatory Gates table + 49-row Gate Registry untouched. TDD RED→GREEN via tests/regression/v8.16.0/F1 + F2 (12 sub-cases). Suite 234/4 (baseline 232 + the 2 new files; the 4 pre-existing Langfuse/env failures unchanged). Iron Law preserved: only `.claude/hooks/step-ledger-stamp.cjs` + `tests/` touched; agents/skills/references/lib untouched (the lib helper was already shipping in v7.14.0). v8.15.1 (2026-06-27, PATCH — audit read-budget dispatch-exemption persistence fix): writeLedger() in lib/audit-read-budget.cjs now preserves the dispatched flag across writes, so the PostToolUse read-counter (bumpLedger) no longer wipes the markDispatched exemption on the first read — a dispatched audit/review subagent stays exempt from the inline read budget for the whole governed window instead of being re-blocked after one read (the gate was starving its own dispatched reviewers, observed live at files_reviewed:0). The flag is read under the same tamper + arm_id guard as the count, so reset-on-new-invocation and tamper-reset still hold. TDD RED→GREEN via tests/regression/v8.14.0/F11 regression block; independent adversarial review 0 critical/0 high/0 medium (1 info: forged unsigned dispatched:true persists — pre-existing cooperative-model posture, closed by PIPELINE_HMAC_STRICT); suite 232/4 (4 pre-existing Langfuse/env); Iron Law preserved, only lib/ + tests/ touched. v8.15.0 (2026-06-26, MINOR — chain-tied enforcement remediation + e2e self-evaluation loop):two additive work-streams shipped together. (1) Chain-tied enforcement: marker reading consolidated into one SSOT lib (lib/arm-pending.cjs) consumed by the arm-gate, the audit-read-budget gate and the new audit-dispatch-marker hook (closes a DRY/DIP violation where the same verify-on-read + TTL logic lived in three places and two hooks borrowed predicates from a sibling hook); the audit read-budget now exempts a genuinely dispatched subagent so legitimate orchestration is never throttled; three final-review security findings fixed — SEC-1 dispatch-mark allowlist (only real review/audit/ux agents mark the ledger, never sentinel/task-orchestrator/brainstorm steps), SEC-3 rehydration deny-message no longer leaks the exec-window write path, SEC-4 workflow label echoed into LLM-facing deny reasons is reconstructed from a validated MODE/Type allowlist (injection-safe SSOT in lib/pipeline-workflow-classifier.cjs). (2) E2E self-evaluation loop: a deterministic step-by-step evaluator (lib/e2e-evaluator.cjs) over an explicit contract (lib/contracts/e2e-eval.cjs), a gate hook (e2e-eval-gate.cjs), a loop-closer (scripts/e2e-eval-complete.cjs) and a /self-eval command score a finished run and surface human-gated improvement proposals at Stop. KNOWN LIMITATION (cooperative-model floor): enforcement is prompt/coordination-based, not cryptographic against the parent — a parent with .pipeline write access can still un-govern itself by deleting its own coordination state; full closure needs PIPELINE_HMAC_STRICT default-on plugin-wide, a deliberate posture change deferred to a future major. Tests in tests/regression/v8.14.0/ (F1-F13) + tests/regression/v8.15.0/; suite 232/4 (4 pre-existing Langfuse/env). Iron Law preserved: only .claude/hooks/, lib/, hooks.json, commands/, scripts/, tests/ — Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED. v8.11.1 (2026-06-22, PATCH — enforcement deadlock fix): dispatch-pending-gate now exempts the sentinel recovery spawn in both branches (live handshake + corrupt state), mirroring sentinel-hook, closing the irrecoverable deadlock where one gate sends for the sentinel and the other bars it. +tests/regression/v8.12.0/T4 (6 scenarios RED->GREEN); only hooks/ + tests/; suite 206/4 (4 pre-existing Langfuse/env). v8.4.0 (2026-06-18, MINOR — spec-workflow telemetry/fidelity bridge [auditoria audit-heavy AUDIT-001..006]): o workflow de authoring /pipeline-orchestrator:spec (spec-controller, v8.0.0) rodava em pipeline-runs/<run_id>/ mas NUNCA deixava rastro mensurável — zero entradas no run-log, zero traces no Langfuse (confirmado via REST: 50 traces, 0 spec), e a nota de fidelidade usava a régua errada (gates de PROCESSING em vez dos eventos de AUTHORING). Causa: a telemetria descobre runs via .pipeline/active-run.json → sentinel-state.json (fast-path) ou varrendo .pipeline/docs/ (fallback), e o spec-controller não escrevia nenhum dos dois. Correção ADITIVA (sem mover runs, sem tocar o scan .pipeline/docs/ nem a tabela Mandatory Gates): (1) lib/run-directory.cjs allocate() agora publica .pipeline/active-run.json (gravação atômica temp+rename) + sentinel-state.json assinado no run dir, fail-safe; (2) stop-hook.cjs + langfuse-hook.cjs (+ espelhos .codex) descobrem um run selado em pipeline-runs/ via o ponteiro mesmo inativo; (3) novo lib/run-seal.cjs sealSpecRun() atualiza manifest (novo status 'sealed'), fecha o sentinel (pipeline_active:false) e grava SPEC_SEALED em gate-decisions.jsonl — com CLI entry (node lib/run-seal.cjs <runDir> --variant spec-authoring); (4) fidelity-reporter.cjs régua sensível ao fluxo: authoring={SPEC_SEALED,SPEC_REVIEW_FINDINGS} via allowlist EXATA {spec-authoring,spec-author}, processing inalterado; (5) o stop-hook só honra a variante de authoring (régua menor) se a assinatura HMAC do sentinel conferir (estado forjado cai no conjunto seguro). spec-controller.md Step 0/8/9 amarrado à selagem. Revisão adversarial 3 revisores zero-context em 3 rounds (convergiu sem CRITICAL/HIGH): pegou o bug crítico de a variante não ser repassada no caminho real do teardown (a régua nova nunca dispararia) + downgrade por variante não-verificada + 8 itens menores, todos corrigidos. +tests/regression/v8.4.0/F6 (17 sub-testes) + F3 silent-skip removido; suíte 142/4 (4 Langfuse env pré-existentes); espelhos .codex byte-idênticos. Lockstep 9 superfícies. v8.0.3 (2026-06-17, PATCH — protocol doc guard): adds an explicit anti-pattern note in references/gate-request-protocol.md (parent-side handler) and skills/spec/SKILL.md (Achado #7 handler) — re-dispatch is a fresh Agent() call that re-hydrates state from the run-dir, NOT a live-agent resume; SendMessage is not used by this pipeline and, even where present, is a deferred tool loadable via ToolSearch. Closes the failure mode where the parent main LLM mis-reads an AWAITING_* handshake as needing SendMessage and abandons the run. ZERO runtime change. v8.0.2 (2026-06-16, PATCH — release hygiene): clean npm tarball (drops a leaked runtime session .lock and tracks the previously npm-only /help command; .npmignore hardened for **/.pipeline/ + *.lock; new tests/packaging/no-pack-cruft guard), the Paperclip flow-mirror tests are now wired into the release gate (run-tests.cjs `paperclip` category, 13/13), and the stale description/README prose was refreshed. ZERO runtime change. v8.0.1 (2026-06-15, PATCH — Paperclip layer): reflects the v8.0.0 spec-authoring takeover + fixes the flow-mirror handoff stalls (Montagem Progressiva + structural root-cause guards), additive in references/paperclip, cursor manifest lockstep. v8.0.0 (2026-06-15, MAJOR — spec-authoring takeover): /pipeline-orchestrator:spec now AUTHORS a new specification from scratch (brainstorm clarification + proactive ideation + forced design-interrogation + Kiro lifecycle + adversarial document review + contract seal) and stops before implementing; legacy spec PROCESSING moved to /spec-light, /spec-heavy, /spec-audit-only and /pipeline --type=Spec. Gate Registry 35→43 (8 new AUDIT/SOFT events; the Mandatory Gates by Complexity table is unchanged, F1 preserved). v7.14.1 (2026-06-14, PATCH - gate-decision SSOT shipped / schema-drift fix): entrega a Fase 1 do contract-enforcement ja mergeada em main (commit 4420907) - novo lib/contracts/gate-decision.cjs como fonte unica do vocabulario canonico (8 decisoes + 5 hardness + 13 chaves) consumido por gate-decision-writer + jsonl-sanitizer + fidelity-reporter; encerra o drift que fazia final-validator/pipeline-controller/audit-trail exigirem exatamente 8 chaves e procurarem um RESOLVED nunca emitido, fazendo o plugin reprovar o proprio audit trail de 13 campos. Prosa alinhada ao writer real; novo teste tests/regression/v7.9.0/F-gate-decision-contract (10/10). Auditoria audit-heavy do proprio pipeline (2026-06-14) confirmou base correta v7.14.0, integracao limpa e zero regressao (suite 114/4/118; 4 falhas pre-existentes Langfuse+flaky). Iron Law: quebra minima em agents/lib/commands/references reconhecida e justificada pelo bug de auto-auditoria. v7.10.1 (2026-06-09, PATCH — parallel_eligible default contract): formaliza o default de parallel_eligible como false quando a analise de sobreposicao nao pode ser concluida. plan-architect ganha Regra 5 exigindo parallel_eligible: false com overlap_reason prefixado 'analysis_incomplete:' quando CHANGE_CONTRACT esta ausente ou contem wildcards. executor-controller ganha guarda explicito tratando parallel_eligible ausente como false com WARN trace, convertendo comportamento implicito JS falsy em contrato observavel. 2 cenarios de regressao adicionados (F22-S10 + F22-S11, total 11). Dogfood validou Plan Mode protocol: 3/3 agentes obrigatorios emitiram PLAN_MODE_REQUEST na primeira tentativa. v7.10.0 (2026-06-09, MINOR — Plan Mode generalized + parallel dispatch): generaliza o Plan Mode obrigatorio de 1 para 10 agentes (7 de pesquisa + 2 implementadores + plan-architect) e implementa despacho paralelo de tarefas em lotes MEDIA. Cada agente obrigatorio (step-01-explore, audit-intake, audit-domain-analyzer, bugfix-diagnostic-agent, bugfix-root-cause-analyzer, feature-vertical-slice-planner, design-interrogator, executor-implementer-task, feature-implementer) agora emite PLAN_MODE_REQUEST v1 como Step 0/0b antes de qualquer pesquisa pesada, delegando leitura ao parent em isolamento somente-leitura. Enforcement PLAN_MODE_BYPASS generalizado no pipeline-controller: qualquer agente da tabela PLAN_MODE_MANDATORY_AGENTS que retornar sem ciclo REQUEST/RESULTS dispara AUDIT + re-dispatch unico. Novo campo parallel_eligible nos metadados de batch emitidos pelo plan-architect: quando as tarefas de um lote tem escopos de arquivo disjuntos (CHANGE_CONTRACT sem sobreposicao), o executor-controller despacha N implementadores simultaneamente. checkpoint-validator ganha campo per_task_status condicional para atribuir falhas por tarefa em lotes paralelos. Revisao adversarial com 3 revisores zero-context: 6 fixes aplicados (ADV-001 IMPLEMENTATION_RESULT na lista de saidas substantivas, ADV-002 enforcement delegation clarity, COR-001/002 micro-gate exempt from Plan Mode precondition, COR-003/004 CONTEXT LOADING STRATEGY as delegation definition, ADV-006 conditional per_task_status, COR-005 VSA_PLAN source). 4 novos arquivos de teste em tests/regression/v7.10.0/ (F20 45 cenarios + F21 14 cenarios + F22 9 cenarios + F23 6 cenarios = 74 total). Iron Law preservada: references/gates.md, hooks e lib intocados; 35-gate Registry e 23-row Mandatory Gates untouched. v7.9.4 (2026-06-06, PATCH — plan-mode contract fix): corrige o contrato do Plan Mode que nunca disparava (21+ runs reais sem nenhum PLAN_MODE_REQUEST; modo de falha B5-001). agents/quality/plan-architect.md: Steps 0/3/4 + rodape Tools + 4 claims de header alinhados ao protocolo Achado #7 — o agente agora emite PLAN_MODE_REQUEST (Step 0) e GATE_REQUEST (Step 3) e PARA, em vez de tentar chamar EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode/AskUserQuestion (ferramentas strippadas de subagentes pelo harness); rodape Tools lista apenas Read/Grep/Glob + blocos de protocolo; label da Rule 1 preservado para o contrato D7-S7 v6.1.0. agents/core/pipeline-controller.md: novo enforcement PLAN_MODE_BYPASS — retorno do plan-architect contendo IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN sem ciclo PLAN_MODE_REQUEST/PLAN_MODE_RESULTS gera evento AUDIT em protocol-events.jsonl + um unico re-dispatch; aceita no segundo bypass (sem deadlock em sessoes legadas); NAO e gate novo (registry de 35 e Inline Invariants intocados). 2 testes novos em tests/regression/v7.9.4/ (F18 plan-mode-contract, 8 cenarios com extractor fence-aware provado por mutacao; F19 controller-inline-plan-check, 6 cenarios) pinam o contrato. Dogfood run completo: Bug Fix/MEDIA/bugfix-light, TDD 14 cenarios RED→GREEN, adversarial per-batch (B1/B2 PASS), final adversarial com 3 revisores zero-context (0 critical; 2 importants corrigidos em rework unico, incl. regressao D7-S7 detectada por verificacao direta e reconciliada), Pa de Cal GO @ 0.94; suite 62/67 = baseline pre-run identico (5 falhas Langfuse/env pre-existentes). Iron Law preservada: references/gates.md, hooks e lib intocados. v7.9.2 (2026-06-02, PATCH — documentation refresh): atualiza toda a documentacao de usuario e referencia para refletir as features ate v7.9.1 — README e diagramas saem de v7.4.0/v6.0.0 para v7.9.2 (incl. correcao da licenca MIT->PolyForm Shield nos diagramas), documentacao completa da camada Paperclip (integration layer v7.6.0 + company provisioner v7.8.0 + flow-mirror v7.9.0 com arvore de issues + 8 comandos paperclip-* + flag --on=paperclip + novo references/paperclip/PAPERCLIP-FLOW-MIRROR.md), secao run-log do audit-trail atualizada para o modelo v7.9.1 (buildRunLogEntry derivando total_gates_expected/fidelity_score/final_decision), vocabulario de decisao de gate alinhado aos 8 valores canonicos, e correcao de drift de contagens (gate registry header 22->35, Mandatory table 22->23, suite 61). Docs-only: ZERO mudancas em codigo de runtime; Iron Law preservada. v7.9.1 (2026-06-02, PATCH — telemetry run-log aggregator fix): buildRunLogEntry (.claude/hooks/stop-hook.cjs + .codex mirror) had 4 confirmed defects in the per-session run-log summary. D2: read classification from sentinel.classification_post_orchestrator instead of the source of truth sentinel.orchestrator_decision (type/complexity/variant came out null almost always) — now reads orchestrator_decision (.type/.task_type/.complexity/.pipeline_variant) with legacy fallback. D4: a date-only created_at (2026-06-01, no time) made Date.parse resolve to midnight UTC and inflated duration_seconds to hours — now date-only yields duration_seconds:null. D1: total_gates_expected + fidelity_score were hardcoded null — now total_gates_expected derives from MANDATORY_GATES_BY_COMPLEXITY (MEDIA=11; lazy-required channel-agnostic, soft-fail null) and fidelity_score is read from the run's persisted fidelity-report.json. D3: final_decision fell to literal UNKNOWN even when derivable — now cascades session.status -> sentinel.final_decision -> last CLOSEOUT_CONFIRM in gate-decisions.jsonl before UNKNOWN. TDD: new tests/regression/v7.9.0/F13-runlog-entry-defects.test.cjs (15 scenarios) RED 7-fail -> GREEN 15/15; full suite 61/61; build exit 0. .codex mirror byte-identical (sha256). Option A: scope locked to 2 hooks + 1 test, lib/run-log.cjs untouched (its null->0 coercion is an intentional honest default). Iron Law preserved: ZERO changes to agents/skills/references/commands/lib; Mandatory Gates + Registry untouched. v7.9.0 (2026-06-01, MINOR — Paperclip flow-mirror): espelha os 14 fluxos do pipeline (bugfix/feature/user-story/audit/ux/spec light+heavy + hotfix + review-only) como arvores de issues no Paperclip via novos modulos em references/paperclip/spec/lib/ (tree-template moldes declarativos dos 14 fluxos; tree-factory nextStep/nodeSpec/nodeSpecFanIn/expandSlices com loop de conserto por contador interno + fatias dinamicas; tree-factory-io + grow-tree braco I/O com transport injetavel e guard dry-run/--confirm que NAO toca a producao sem confirmacao; classify-bridge classificacao local tipo+complexidade). Patch na regua mirror-fidelity-dictionary.cjs: CLARIFICATION_DONE -> INFO_GATE_BLOCKED (teto SIMPLES 0.80 -> 1.0) + modo REVIEW_ONLY com lista fixa de portoes. 8 novos comandos slash commands/paperclip-{bugfix,feature,user-story,audit,ux,spec,hotfix,review}.md + paperclip-overview.md: cada um classifica local, monta a arvore em dry-run e SO cria os cartoes na VPS apos confirmacao explicita (AskUserQuestion); flag --on=paperclip documentada em commands/pipeline.md. Construido com DDD+ATDD+BDD+TDD + revisao adversarial em loop por etapa (72 achados corrigidos); suite mirror-fidelity 63 -> 309 GREEN. Aditivo: os comandos paperclip-* sao canal paralelo de despacho, ZERO mudancas que quebrem fluxos do Claude Code; Iron Law respeitada nos agents/skills/references originais. v7.8.0 (2026-05-26, MINOR - Paperclip company provisioner): ships references/paperclip/scripts/provision-pipeline-company.cjs, an ID-agnostic Node provisioner that stands up a Paperclip company with the full 47-cargo pipeline-orchestrator roster + 11 custom skills (heartbeat OFF — inert until an issue is assigned). Resolves the company by name (creates if absent), captures agent IDs at runtime, computes all plugin/skill/workflow paths from __dirname (no hardcoded UUIDs or machine paths), and wires the 12 category->skill + category->workflow-spec mappings from PLAN-46-AGENTS-UPDATE. Referenced by the /pipeline-orchestrator:setup-paperclip command as the programmatic provisioning step. Global OpenCode commands now include audit/UX/SPEC light and heavy flows. v7.6.1 (2026-05-24, PATCH — release hygiene): CLAUDE.md canonical version line bumped 7.5.0 → 7.6.0 (was lagging since v7.6.0 release), .cursor-plugin/plugin.json description re-synced to match .claude-plugin/plugin.json (was lagging at v7.4.1 desc), tests/regression/v7.1.0/F7_version_sync.cjs VERSION constant updated 7.5.0 → 7.6.0 then to 7.6.1 (rolled forward per release convention). Suite 58/59 GREEN (1 pre-existing F2 langfuse vendoring failure documented and accepted as v7.4.2 baseline). ZERO runtime/agent/skill/reference/command changes — purely release hygiene closure of doc drift detected by F7 during v7.6.0 npm publish. v7.6.0 (2026-05-22, MINOR — Paperclip integration layer): adiciona references/paperclip/ com 14 documentos + 11 skills custom que permitem rodar o plugin pipeline-orchestrator dentro do Paperclip (open-source agent orchestration platform — paperclip.ing) via adapter claude_local com paridade efetiva ao Claude Code standalone. Inclui PAPERCLIP-AXIOMS.md (contrato inquebravel + 4 axiomas), 6 workflow specs prompt-native (PAPERCLIP-{BUGFIX,FEATURE,AUDIT,UX,SPEC,ADVERSARIAL}-WORKFLOW.md), 11 skills wrappers (engineering-principles canonica + 10 pipeline-orchestrator-*), paperclip-kb.md (KB operacional — heartbeat 9 steps, skills system, PARA memory, adapter config codex_local + claude_local, API endpoints), paperclip-catalog.md (46 agentes mapeados com 8 campos cada), paperclip-adaptation-spec.md (traducao Achado #7 para modelo Paperclip), ZONING.md (regra D: canonical vs C: install vs workspace local), PLAN-46-AGENTS-UPDATE.md (procedimento de migracao com loop adversarial 7 batches), pilot-information-gate.md (validacao end-to-end PARITY_VERDICT=PASS_WITH_NOTES — leu agent.md original, GATE_REQUEST v1 schema confirmado, 22 skills detectadas, 4 references auxiliares lidas), install-junctions.bat (deployment de skills via mklink /J Windows), novo slash command /pipeline-orchestrator:setup-paperclip (orquestra deteccao + install + configuracao end-to-end) e nova doc docs/PAPERCLIP-INTEGRATION.md (visao geral arquitetura + setup + gaps conhecidos). Iron Law respeitado: ZERO mudancas em agents/, skills/ existentes, references/ originais (paperclip/ eh subdir adicional), commands/ originais ou lib/. 23-row Mandatory Gates table e 35-row Registry untouched. Backward compat 100%. v7.5.0 (2026-05-22, MINOR — concurrent-safe tracing): isolates concurrent pipeline runs sharing a working directory so trace/span carriers, manifest allocation, and sentinel state discovery no longer collide on PPID or mtime heuristics. lib/run-directory.cjs::allocate switches the primary mkdir from {recursive:true} to {recursive:false} and retries on EEXIST with a fresh collision-resistant uniqueId (Date.now base36 + crypto.randomBytes(6).hex). The resulting runId — `${ordinal}-${uniqueId}-${slug}` — is published on process.env.PIPELINE_RUN_ID so descendant processes (langfuse-hook, sentinel-hook, stop-hook) key per-run state off the env-var instead of process.ppid or sentinel-state.json mtime. All three hooks fall back gracefully when PIPELINE_RUN_ID is absent, emitting one-shot AUDIT events (CARRIER_PPID_FALLBACK / DISCOVERY_RUN_ID_NO_MATCH / DISCOVERY_MTIME_FALLBACK) on stderr so the degradation is observable. New PIPELINE_TRACING_SCOPE env-var (`agent-only` default = v7.4.0 parity / `agent-plus-skill` / `full`) replaces the silent `tool !== 'Agent'` filter at langfuse-hook entry-point; unknown values fall through to safe-default with AUDIT TRACING_SCOPE_UNKNOWN. Codex mirror kept byte-identical per Iron Law. 5 new regression tests in tests/regression/v7.5.0/ (F1-F5, 44 sub-assertions, 100% GREEN). Existing unit tests in tests/unit/run-directory.test.js updated to the new runId format (external_consumers=none per spec.json). Backward-compat preserved: legacy single-arg PPID callers get the fallback path + AUDIT breadcrumb. v7.4.2 (2026-05-22, PATCH — Langfuse SDK vendored: bundles langfuse@3.38.4 + langfuse-core@3.38.20 + mustache@4.2.0 into the package so the cache works without a global install; closes Cannot find module 'langfuse' in marketplace cache). Automated multi-agent pipeline. v7.2.0 (2026-05-21) — MINOR. Phase 0 hardening: (1) STATE_FILE_INIT_FAIL gate (CIRCUIT_BREAKER) — hard precondition on the initial sentinel-state.json write, with happy-path PASS entry to preserve fidelity score; (2) PROTOCOL_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT gate (HARD) — two-layer detection (primary out-of-band hook + secondary controller re-entry check) for stale GATE_REQUEST/DISPATCH_REQUEST/PLAN_MODE_REQUEST blocks; default 30min, env override PIPELINE_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS with 60000ms floor; closes Achado #7 silent-deadlock pattern; (3) cleanup-orphan-sentinel-state-hook (.claude/hooks/, SessionStart) — auto-archives sentinel-state.json files older than 24h via atomic rename to sentinel-state.archived-<ts>.json (closes Achado B1-001); sandbox-checked cwd, depth-limited walk, symlink-skip; (4) --strict-spec flag — rejects Signal 3 (prose regex) and Signal 4 (glob fallback) Spec detection in unattended/CI mode where AskUserQuestion is unavailable; preserves Signal 1 (explicit path) and Signal 2 (--type=spec); precedence over conflicting FORCE_VARIANT=spec-*; STRICT_SPEC_REJECTION gate (AUDIT) logged. Gate Registry 32 → 35 rows (Core 16→18 + Routing 3→4). Mandatory Gates 22 → 23 (+STATE_FILE_INIT_FAIL). 4 batches with ATDD/BDD per scenario, TDD red→green per file, adversarial review per batch (22 findings addressed: 3 CRITICAL fixed, 12 HIGH fixed). Suite 45/46 green (F7 version-sync updated). v7.1.1 (2026-05-21) — PATCH (docs only). Enriches docs/diagrams/pipeline-overview.html with a new "Fluxos detalhados ponta a ponta" section covering 7 workflows end-to-end (bugfix light/heavy, audit light/heavy, UX sim light/heavy, plan, brainstorm, hotfix, review-only) with 12 inline SVG step diagrams (agents + gates color-coded) and a full Thariq-style interactive layer (sticky TOC pills, light/heavy filter pills, per-flow review checkboxes with localStorage persistence, per-flow Copy summary buttons, collapsible exemplo cards, response form with 11-option radio + notes + Save/Copy/Clear actions, 7/7 review counter). ZERO changes to agents/, skills/, references/, commands/, hooks/, lib/ or tests/ — pure documentation enrichment. Iron Law preserved: 22-row Mandatory Gates table and 27-row Registry untouched. v7.1.0 (2026-05-19) — MINOR. Telemetry hygiene: new lib/gate-decision-writer.cjs SSOT writer with the canonical 8-value decision vocabulary (BLOCKED, DISPATCHED, SKIPPED, APPROVED, CONFIRMED, REJECTED, TRIGGERED, NOT_TRIGGERED) — runtime throws TypeError on unknown values (defense-in-depth paired with diff-discipline-reviewer's new SSOT-bypass check). Every event auto-carries run_id/plugin_version/schema_version/type/complexity correlation envelope so cross-run analytics no longer require folder-path joins. New .claude/hooks/stop-hook.cjs (registered in .claude/settings.json) calls lib/run-log.cjs::appendRunLog on every Claude Code session end including crashes — closes the 1-line-per-20-runs aggregate bug (Finding #6). lib/fidelity-reporter.cjs VALID_HARDNESS gains AUDIT (was missing since v6.2.0); VALID_DECISION widened to all 8 canonical values; validate*() now pushes a warning to fidelity-report's warnings[] when an invalid value is encountered (was silently nullified). 7 regression tests in tests/regression/v7.1.0/ (F1-F7). 4 agent prose files updated to reference the canonical vocabulary. Backward compat: historical events still tolerated as '(invalid)' (no migration); 22-row Mandatory Gates table and 27-row Registry untouched. Deferred to v7.2.0: gate rename (CHECKPOINT_FAIL/PLAN_REJECTED for clarity) and timestamp format normalization. v7.0.0 (2026-05-19) — MAJOR (license-only). Licensed under PolyForm Shield 1.0.0 (source-available, noncompete clause). All v7.0.0 code is identical to v6.3.0; the major bump signals license-only change per SemVer convention. See LICENSE and NOTICE.md. v6.3.0 (2026-05-19) — MINOR. Implementation Discipline Layer: new SSOT `references/implementation-discipline.md` (scope control, minimal diff, SOLID/KISS/DRY/YAGNI, dependency/config/contract/migration restrictions, test integrity, evidence requirements, severity PASS/NEEDS_REDUCTION/REJECTED). plan-architect emits `CHANGE_CONTRACT` inline in IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN (allowed_files, allowed_new_files, forbidden_files, forbidden_change_types, diff_budget, escalation_required_if, bootstrap). executor-implementer-task gains SCOPE LOCK CHECK section that fires before every Write/Edit (Read remains free; on violation returns status=QUESTIONS with question.context=scope_lock_violation). New agent `agents/quality/diff-discipline-reviewer.md` (tools=Read,Grep,Glob only; static-only inspection; verdict PASS/NEEDS_REDUCTION/REJECTED; max_fix_attempts=5 independent from ADVERSARIAL_BLOCK max=3). review-orchestrator wires diff-discipline as third parallel reviewer alongside adversarial-batch + architecture-reviewer with dual fix_loop_counters. architecture-reviewer adds 5 new checks (abstraction without second use case, new file where local change suffices, unnecessary architecture layer, unrelated refactor, public contract drift) plus 2 reused existing checks (duplicated helper/type/constant, semantic duplication). 7 regression tests in tests/regression/v6.3.0/ (F8-F14). 22-row Mandatory Gates by Complexity invariant preserved (NO new gate added to that table; F1 untouched); Registry stays at 27 rows (F14 defense-in-depth confirms). Bootstrap exception: this release itself created the very enforcement machinery — T1-T3 of v6.3.0 ran without SCOPE LOCK CHECK enforcement (mechanism being born); from T4 onward in this release and all subsequent plans, enforcement is active. Bootstrap flag is one-time-only and locks itself in v6.4.0+. v6.2.0 (2026-05-18) — MINOR. Pre-execution clarification overhaul: replaces fixed 7-question brainstorm template with a 4-phase dynamic agent (Phase A reads project context + likely-affected files BEFORE any question; Phase B inventories gaps across 11 lenses + domain extensions for auth/payment/PII/migration/concurrency; Phase C asks one GATE_REQUEST per real gap with mandatory file:line evidence in option descriptions; Phase D records 02-explore.md + emits structured JSONL telemetry). Adds new step-01b-alternatives agent (proposes 2-4 alternative approaches across Minimal/Pattern-aligned/Aggressive/Contrarian axes after clarification, auto-skips for mechanical tasks). Drift fix on references/complexity-matrix.md: 'Has spec' column for MEDIA changed from 'Optional' to 'Required (auto-dispatch)', matching pipeline-controller.md:281 runtime behavior. Adds 5 new gates (CLARIFICATION_GAPS_DETECTED, CLARIFICATION_RESOLVED, CLARIFICATION_SKIPPED, ALTERNATIVES_PROPOSED, ALTERNATIVE_CHOSEN) plus new AUDIT hardness level (informational telemetry, never blocks, excluded from fidelity scoring). Gate registry 22 → 27. Brainstorm controller step table grows 9 → 10. Dogfood evidence preserved in docs/audits/2026-05-18-clarification-overhaul-dogfood/ (real MEDIA scenario against IFRS 16 contracts.py:232 list_contracts; 14 telemetry events; 4 anti-invention blockers surfaced — streaming/PII/cap/timeout — that the legacy 7-Q template would have left for downstream invention). Backward compat preserved (legacy pipeline-runs/ resume cleanly via missing-field handling on notes.options.alternatives_done). v6.1.0 (2026-05-15) — MINOR. ATDD/BDD/DDD workflow contracts: D5 ATDD non-spec Given/When/Then in quality-gate-router Step 1b, D6 BDD Gherkin per slice in feature-vertical-slice-planner Step 3b, D7 DDD lightweight Bounded Contexts in plan-architect with SOFT BOUNDED_CONTEXT_MISSING event. v6.0.0 (2026-05-15) — MAJOR RELEASE. Comprehensive audit + hardening: 12/12 CRITICAL findings resolved, 22/22 registry gates exercised in runtime, 16 subagents declare Achado #7 protocol explicitly, 3 new security mitigations (HMAC sentinel-state signature, JSONL sanitization parser, pipeline.local.md sandbox parser), 39 regression tests, audit reports archived in docs/audits/2026-05-15-ifrs16-deep-audit/. Migration: hooks consumers should switch to new names; no behavioral change. v5.2.0 (2026-05-08, promoted from v5.2.0-rc.2 with same content; user authorized direct promotion for distribution to all marketplace consumers). v5.2.0-rc.2 (2026-05-08) — Achado #7 protocol adoption expanded to 5 SKILLs (audit/bugfix/feature/spec/pipeline) + brainstorm-controller step agents (step-00-intake, step-01-explore via DISPATCH_REQUEST) + 9 pipeline-controller dispatch sites (Phase 0a/0b/0c, 1.5, 2c/2e, 3a/3b/3c + sentinel) with concrete YAML examples. Closes 3 protocol-coverage lacunas surfaced post-rc.1 adversarial verification. +4 contract regression tests; suite 294 PASS / 10 FAIL (pre-existing Windows CRLF) / 1 SKIPPED. Zero regressions. v5.2.0-rc.1 (2026-05-07) — Achados 1-7 reconciliation + GATE_REQUEST/DISPATCH_REQUEST protocol. Closes 7 contract drift findings from a v5.1.0 dogfood adversarial review. Achados 1-6 are documentation reconciliation (Phase 1.5 plan-mode trigger across 5 surfaces, brainstorm flag propagation, STEP 1.7 classification guard, type=Spec coverage). Achado 7 is architectural: empirical probe confirmed Claude Code harness strips AskUserQuestion/Agent/EnterPlanMode from subagent tool manifests at runtime, regardless of frontmatter; mitigation via emit-and-hoist protocol (3 new block schemas in references/gate-request-protocol.md), adopted in pipeline-controller, brainstorm-controller, executor-controller. Protocol entries live in protocol-events.jsonl (separate from gate-decisions.jsonl) to avoid final-validator's strict 22-gate-registry validation; named gates dual-write for cross-traceability. +13 contract regression tests (suite stays GREEN, zero regressions). 5 follow-ups deferred to v5.2 final: state persistence, lock cleanup, runtime tests, dispatch_id retry, exec-window protocol migration. v5.0.0 — first major release, promoted from v5.0.0-rc.1 with same content. All 7 v5.0 DoD criteria DONE; see CHANGELOG.md [5.0.0] for the promotion summary and [4.17.0] for the technical Wave 8-spec content. v4.17.0 (Wave 8-spec — TRACE.md schema + writer + standalone validator + plan-mode auto-trigger + --no-plan bypass): closes 3 v5.0 DoD criteria in one wave (#2 TRACE.md by default, #3 attachable to PR, #6 plan-mode-enforcer auto-triggers on MEDIA/COMPLEXA). Modifies `agents/core/pipeline-controller.md` Phase 3 closure with new Step 3b-post (TRACE.md emitter wired between final-validator and finishing-branch; opens edit-guard exec-window for cross-tree write to `.pipeline-orchestrator/runs/<run-id>/`); modifies `commands/pipeline.md` Phase 1.5 trigger from `complexity == COMPLEXA` to `complexity ∈ {MEDIA, COMPLEXA} AND --no-plan ausente` plus new `--no-plan` row in modes table; adds asymmetric override semantics (MEDIA + --no-plan = skip+log; COMPLEXA + --no-plan = plan runs anyway, override logged). Corrects `designs/pipeline-orchestrator-v5-consolidated.md` §8 prose to remove ambiguity flagged in `docs/v5-readiness-report.md` §1. Iron Law respected: ZERO changes to gate registry (still 22 in `references/gates.md`), sentinel checkpoints, or agent dispatch namespace; default behavior (no `--no-plan`) preserves v4.16.0 EXACTLY for SIMPLES and COMPLEXA; MEDIA gets plan-architect for the FIRST TIME (additive behavior change, documented in CHANGELOG Behavior Changes section). Suite stays GREEN: 149+17 NEW unit + 7 NEW integration + 5/1 compat + hooks syntax. Wave 8-spec self-hosted dogfood revealed sentinel-hook cwd-discovery brittleness (logged as v5.0-rc.1 follow-up). v4.16.0 (Wave 8-pre / Issue #11 — Real-mode Compat Runner): closes Issue #11 by reimplementing `tests/compat/runner.cjs::executeReal()` from stub to working real-mode CI harness using the `PreToolUse` hook contract that became available in Claude Code v2.1.85+ (`permissionDecision: "allow"` + `updatedInput.answers` to satisfy `AskUserQuestion` in headless mode). Adds `tests/compat/auto-answer-hook.cjs` (generic first-option-default hook with header-keyed overrides via `tests/compat/v4-baseline/answers-overrides.json`), `tests/unit/compat-runner-real.test.js` (8 hook unit tests + 1 conditional real-mode test that skips when claude CLI absent), and splits `.github/workflows/compat-regression.yml` into mock job (always, gate of PR) + real job (opt-in via `vars.CLAUDE_CLI_AVAILABLE=true` + `secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, runs bugfix-fixture smoke on PR or `--all` on `workflow_dispatch`). Updates `designs/slice-0/SPIKE-CI-HARNESS.md` from PARTIAL PASS to PASS. Iron Law respected: ZERO changes to `agents/`, `skills/`, `references/`, `commands/`, `hooks/` (plugin core untouched). Suite stays GREEN: 129 unit + 8 NEW unit + 12 integration + 5 PASS / 1 SKIPPED compat + 8 hooks syntax (exit 0). DoD criterion #5 advances PARTIAL → PARTIAL+. Wave 8 (TRACE.md + plan-mode auto-trigger) remains the next gate to v5.0.0-rc.1. v4.15.0 (Wave 7-spec — v4-to-v5 Migration Doc + v5 Readiness Report): docs-only release that consolidates the v4.0 → v4.14 lineage into `docs/MIGRATION-v4-to-v5.md` (10 sections: lineage table, 13 accumulated breaking changes, FQDN mapping for the v4.9.0 namespace correction, DoD v5.0 status with 3 DONE / 2 PARTIAL / 2 OPEN, compat matrix for FX Studio + Pulsar + custom forks, what v5.0 will/won't change) and adds `docs/v5-readiness-report.md` (1–2-page snapshot at v4.15.0 with the 7-criterion checklist + verification methods + gap analysis + recommended path: Issue #11 → Wave 8 (TRACE.md + plan-mode auto-trigger) → tag v5.0.0-rc.1). Iron Law respected: ZERO changes to agents/, skills/, references/, commands/, hooks/, or tests/. Suite stays GREEN at v4.14.0 levels: 129 unit + 12 integration + 5 PASS / 1 SKIPPED compat + 8 hooks syntax (exit 0). v4.14.0 (Wave 6-spec — Integration Tests + Compat Fixture + Bad-Variant Fixtures): pure additive release that hardens the Wave 1–5 spec lifecycle stack with three layers of net. Adds `tests/integration/spec-workflow-e2e.test.js` (8 new scenarios) covering the spec-format-gate → spec-content-reviewer → spec-post-impl-validator → spec-closer chain, gate hardness/recovery assertions against `references/gates.md` (22-gate registry), `mode_used` mapping in `agents/executor/spec-closer.md` (spec-heavy → full / spec-light → slim / spec-audit-only → audit), and ADVERSARIAL_LOOP_CHECKPOINT counter-reset semantics from v4.13.0 (`shouldEscalate(attempt, batch_changed)` pure function). Adds TEMPLATE compat fixture `tests/compat/v4-baseline/scenarios/spec-fixture/` (input.md + expected.yaml; no mock-output.json) staking out the spec-heavy observable contract for a future dogfooding session — runner.cjs auto-discovers and SKIPs it via the case-insensitive TEMPLATE check. Adds three bad-variant spec fixtures under `tests/fixtures/specs/`: `auth-flow-bad-yaml/` (intentionally unparseable spec.json → SPEC_FORMAT_GATE_FAIL HARD via critical-artifact rule), `auth-flow-no-traceability/` (design.md stripped of Traces-to + Glossary + responsibilities + data model + contracts → ≥8 of 25 checks fail → score ≤17 → NO-GO), `auth-flow-missing-artifact/` (tasks.md absent → SPEC_ARTIFACT_MISSING MANDATORY before format scoring runs). One surgical edit in `tests/unit/compat-baselines.test.js` relaxes a hard-coded SKIPPED regex (0 → 0 or 1) to accommodate the new TEMPLATE fixture, with comment pointing to the future tighten-back trigger. Iron Law respected: ZERO changes to agents/, skills/, references/, commands/, hooks/. Suite: 129 unit + 12 integration (was 4) + 5 PASS / 1 SKIPPED compat, all exit 0. v4.13.0 (Gates Invariants & Mode Persistence — Wave 5-spec): adds 6 spec gates to references/gates.md registry (SSOT sync with pipeline-controller.md Inline Invariants, closing ARCH-1 gap from v4.11.0 adversarial review); extends commands/pipeline.md Inline Invariants from 15 to 21 gate names; adds Spec row to complexity-matrix.md Pipeline Routing Matrix; introduces mode_used field (slim|full|audit) to spec-context-schema.md as spec.json field; wires mode_used write step into spec-closer.md based on pipeline_variant. +16 unit tests (gates registry parsing, escalation rule, variant→mode mapping, regression baseline). v4.12.0 (Wave 4-spec — /spec entry-point + 3 pipeline composition refs): adds the thin manual-only `skills/spec/SKILL.md` entry-point (mirrors skills/bugfix, skills/audit, skills/feature pattern), pre-classifying `task_type=Spec` to skip the type-detection round in task-orchestrator. Variant flags `--light` / `--heavy` / `--audit-only` route directly to skills/spec-light, spec-heavy, or spec-audit-only with their prescriptive 6/9/5-step procedures (Wave 2). Without a flag, dispatches to pipeline-controller, which uses the Wave 3-spec 4-signal classifier to select the right variant. Three new pipeline composition reflexes (`references/pipelines/spec-light.md`, `spec-heavy.md`, `spec-audit-only.md`) document team composition + step-by-step flow with 1:1 fidelity to their SKILL.md sequence_lock — enforced by a new cross-ref test (`tests/unit/spec-entry-point-and-pipelines.test.js`, +8 tests, suite 103→111). Backward compat preserved: `/pipeline-orchestrator:pipeline` full classifier path is unchanged; existing entry-points (bugfix/audit/feature) untouched. Zero changes to agents/, no changes to skills/spec-light, skills/spec-heavy, skills/spec-audit-only, references/gates.md, or commands/pipeline.md (purely additive). v4.11.0 (Wave 3-spec — Spec Classifier Routing): wires type=Spec first-class classification into task-orchestrator (4-signal detection: explicit path / --type=spec flag / prose regex / glob fallback), routes spec-light/spec-heavy/spec-audit-only via pipeline-controller skill dispatch (mirrors bugfix pattern), adds 5 spec sentinel checkpoints (guarded on pipeline_variant.startsWith("spec-")), AC-seeded ATDD in quality-gate-router (1 scenario per AC, EARS preserved + bullet normalized), GO/CONDITIONAL/NO-GO vs PASS/FAIL/SKIPPED vocabulary doc in audit-trail.md. Backward compat preserved (non-spec pipelines route exactly as v4.10.0); zero changes to Wave 1 spec lifecycle agents or Wave 2 spec skill trees. v4.10.0 (Wave 3 — Compat Baselines): graduates all 5 compat fixtures (audit/bugfix/feature/hotfix/ux) from TEMPLATE → REAL baselines captured 2026-05-05 against pipeline v4.10.0. Each fixture now ships paired `expected.yaml` + `mock-output.json` with the 5 observable contract fields (classification, gates_triggered, agents_invoked, artifacts_produced, verdict). Real-mode wiring of the runner remains a Slice 4-extended item: `executeReal` is still a stub due to upstream `--ci-auto-confirm` gap in Claude Code (documented in `designs/slice-0/SPIKE-CI-HARNESS.md`). v4.9.x: spec workflow polish + CI debt. v4.9.0 (Spec Workflow Import — Wave 2): imports 3 prescriptive skill trees. v4.8.0 (Hook Enforcement). v4.7.0 (Slice 3b polish). v4.6.x (Slice 3b feature workflow). v4.5.0 (Slice 3a audit). v4.4.x (Slice 1.5 bugfix import). v4.3.x (skill migration). v4.2.x (SEC patches + thin entry-points). v4 base: orchestration in isolated controller agent.

entry.title: maestro
entry.content: Maestro Frontier is an opt-in, zero-dependency local multi-CLI fusion engine for AI coding agents. Use /maestro:frontier catalog to inspect the portable catalog, then /maestro:frontier compose --models ... to choose and arm a panel. Panel, judge, and synthesizer subprocesses are one-shot and read-only. Off, single, and fusion modes also switch via /maestro:frontier. Built on Maestro orchestration discipline: decision-gated routing, verified done-claims, surgical scope, and structural enforcement hooks.

entry.title: wicked-garden
entry.content: Your coding agent's harness already plans and swarms; Wicked Garden is the curated toolkit for what it can't do alone. Proof, not claims — gates re-derive 'done' through the internal loom engine (fail-closed, independently attested via wicked-vault), so a self-asserted pass can't lie its way green. Relationships grep can't see — codegraph + injected bus/dispatch/capability edges power blast-radius & lineage. Plus deterministic multi-file refactor (wicked-patch), cross-session memory (wicked-brain), real multi-model second opinions (jam:council), portable expertise as on-demand skill-refs, and evidence-gated testing (wicked-testing). It reads the shape of your work to apply the right rigor — steering, not a pipeline — then gets out of the way. The compiler stamps a self-contained evidence gate into any repo that runs with no wicked-garden installed. Don't fight the harness; fill its gaps. The evidence gate requires wicked-vault (installed automatically via wicked-testing); wicked-testing, wicked-brain, wicked-understanding, and wicked-bus are opt-in layers — install what you'll use, the toolkit works without the rest.

entry.title: pinta-cc
entry.content: Generic OTLP forwarder for Claude Code hook events. Converts hook events into OTLP/HTTP spans and sends them to any OpenTelemetry-compatible collector.

entry.title: odoo-ai-skills
entry.content: The local-first verification & deploy gate for AI-written Odoo 17/18/19 changes — static indexes suggest; this verifies against the running instance. Read ground truth from the live instance first (fields, MRO/super chains, views, security, runtime flow, runtime state + exception post-mortem, reverse field impact, effective per-user/company security, module load state), build the smallest correct change, prove it with tests, and review it before merge — never guess. Layer-K discovery solves the cold-start problem — rank the live entrypoint surface (buttons/crons/automations/routes), sample the real cross-app flow as an Execution Surface Graph, measure hallucination reduction against a benchmark, and ENFORCE no-introspect-no-edit via a PreToolUse hook. Layer-I enforcement gates turn that evidence into proof: risk-based scenario test generation, dev-vs-prod environment parity, a static anti-pattern validator, enforced privacy redaction, an upgrade/migration harness, a deploy approval gate, a one-command evidence bundle (PR-comment verdict), and a bring-your-own-index claim verifier; plus a local Odoo developer-docs index (Layer J). No SaaS, no seats, no metadata leaves the box. Covers models, scaffolding, views, OWL, public web (controllers/website/portal), security, reports, data, migration, performance, deploy (incl. odoo.sh), and domain playbooks.

See CHANGELOG.md for per-version history.

entry.title: envisible
entry.content: Manage encrypted secrets inline in config and env files with ENC[...] markers. Bundles a skill that teaches the agent to set up envisible end-to-end: discover plaintext secrets, pick a key mode (local NaCl keypair or GCP/AWS/Azure KMS), encrypt, and wire decryption into the runtime, CI, and git.
